headline: Charlie Munger Once Recalled Childhood Marbles Strategy That Shaped His Investing Mindset For Life: 'I Would Never Play For Stakes Unless…'
summary: Charlie Munger showed how a childhood marble strategy of only betting when he had the edge evolved into a lifelong investing philosophy.

headline: Trump Accelerates Psychedelic Research With $50 Million Ibogaine Commitment
summary: On Saturday, President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to move faster on reviews tied to psychedelic-based research and treatment, including ibogaine that veteran advocates say may help post-traumatic stress disorder.

headline: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Slams US For 'Maximalist' Demands
summary: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh has said that Tehran is not prepared to restart in-person negotiations with U.S. officials, arguing Washington is sticking to "maximalist" terms that block progress.

headline: Elon Musk Says 'If AI/Robots Increase Output, Then You Must Issue Dollars To People or There Will Be Massive Disinflation'
summary: Elon Musk has argued that federally funded checks delivering a universal high income are his preferred response to job losses tied to artificial intelligence, framing it as an inflation-safe policy if automation expands real output fast enough.

headline: Warren Buffett's Autograph Now Costs 63% Of What He Paid For His House — And He's Lived There Since 1958
summary: A rare Warren Buffett autograph went for more than expected, proving that his retirement may put his memorabilia more in demand.

headline: Robin Brooks Warns Europe Must Block Russian Oil Exports To Impact War Financing
summary: In a recent post on X, Robin Brooks argued Europe has the tools to squeeze Russia's war finances by stopping seaborne crude shipments leaving the Baltic, saying a U.S.-style cutoff has a track record of forcing exporters to bend.

summary: Sundar Pichai once praised Musk's ability to turn futuristic ideas into real products, leading to admiration from other tech leaders.

headline: Iran Shuts Strait of Hormuz Again as US Blockade Intensifies and Vessels Report Gunfire
summary: On Saturday, the Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said in a statement that the Strait of Hormuz was shifted back to how it operated before April 17, putting the passage under tighter military oversight after what it described as U.S. interference with shipping.

headline: Strait of Hormuz Closed Again, Iran Says 'Under the Strict Management and Control of the Armed Forces'
summary: Iran's military announced that the strait had "returned to its previous state" and "is under the strict management and control of the Armed Forces."
